_notes: Source: iit-proj-encounter (clinical research, study visit tracking). Target: hemas-encounter (hospital admissions). Source bans hospitalization and requires a study-visit type slice. Target adds hospital extensions (bedNumber, wardName), lifts hospitalization ban, adds new mandatory constraints (participant min=1, reasonCode min=1), and replaces study-specific slicing with clinical encounter bindings.

drifts.description: Source constraints removed: [iit-enc-1 (error: VN identifier required), iit-enc-2 (error: subject must exist), iit-enc-3 (warning: finished encounter needs period.start and period.end)]. Not present in hemas-encounter.
New error constraint added in target: hemas-enc-1 (error: status='finished' implies period.end must exist). Source has no equivalent constraint at error severity.
New warning constraint added in target: hemas-enc-2 (warning: emergency class implies priority must exist). Source has no equivalent constraint.
New error constraint added in target: hemas-enc-3 (error: inpatient class implies location must exist). Source has no equivalent constraint.
Mandatory extension studyVisitNumber[1..1 mustSupport=true] present in source is removed in target. Source required this extension; hemas-encounter does not define it.
Optional extension protocolDeviation[0..* mustSupport=true] present in source is removed in target.
Optional extension visitWindow[0..1 mustSupport=true] present in source is removed in target.
New optional extension bedNumber[0..1 mustSupport=true] added in target. Source does not define this extension.
New optional extension wardName[0..1 mustSupport=true] added in target. Source does not define this extension.
New optional extension attendingConsultant[0..1 mustSupport=true] added in target. Source does not define this extension.

Identifier slicing renamed: source has slice named 'visitId[1..1 mustSupport=true]' with VN pattern on type; target has equivalent slice named 'admissionNumber[1..1 mustSupport=true]' with the same VN pattern on type. Underlying constraint is equivalent but slice name differs.
identifier.type pattern value changed: source uses iit-proj VN type pattern → target uses hemas admission-number type pattern (different system URIs in the CodeableConcept pattern).

identifier.system fixed value changed: source fixed value 'https://iit-proj.example.com/fhir/sid/visit-id' → target fixed value 'https://hemas.example.com/fhir/sid/admission-number'. Different system URIs between iit-proj and hemas.

type slicing removed in target: source has named slice studyVisit[1..1 mustSupport=true patternCC(system=https://iit-proj.example.com/fhir/CodeSystem/encounter-type, code=study-visit)]. Target has no type slicing; replaces slicing with a binding.
New binding added on Encounter.type in target: extensible(https://hemas.example.com/fhir/ValueSet/encounter-type). Source had no binding on type (only slicing).

New MustSupport=true added on serviceType with new binding in target: extensible(https://hemas.example.com/fhir/ValueSet/service-type). Source does not constrain serviceType. Optional element in R4 base.

New MustSupport=true added on priority with new binding in target: extensible(https://hemas.example.com/fhir/ValueSet/encounter-priority). Source does not constrain priority. Optional element in R4 base.
participant min cardinality strictly increased: source [0..* mustSupport=true] (R4 base min=0) → target [1..* mustSupport=true]. At least one participant now mandatory in hemas.

reasonCode min cardinality strictly increased: source [0..* mustSupport=true] (R4 base min=0, no explicit min in source differential) → target [1..* mustSupport=true]. At least one reason code now mandatory in hemas.
diagnosis.condition MustSupport added in target: source has diagnosis[mustSupport=true] but does not constrain diagnosis.condition sub-element; target adds mustSupport=true on diagnosis.condition.
New MustSupport=true added on diagnosis.use with new binding in target: extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/diagnosis-role). Source does not constrain diagnosis.use.

hospitalization ban lifted and MustSupport added: source [0..0] (completely banned) → target [0..* mustSupport=true] (hemas no explicit min, inherits R4 base [0..1]). Ban removed; MS added on optional element.

New MustSupport=true with new binding on hospitalization.admitSource in target: extensible(https://hemas.example.com/fhir/ValueSet/admit-source). Source had no hospitalization constraints (was banned).

New MustSupport=true with new binding on hospitalization.dischargeDisposition in target: extensible(https://hemas.example.com/fhir/ValueSet/discharge-disposition). Source had no hospitalization constraints (was banned).

New hospitalization sub-elements constrained in target: preAdmissionIdentifier[mustSupport=true] and dietPreference[mustSupport=true] gain MustSupport. Source had no hospitalization constraints (banned). These are optional elements with MS added.

location.status MustSupport added in target: source has location[mustSupport=true] and location.location[mustSupport=true] but does not constrain location.status; target adds mustSupport=true on location.status.

Reference target profile changed: source constrains serviceProvider to [iit-proj/Organization] → target constrains to [hemas/Organization]. Both are MS references but pointing to different hospital profiles.
